fingerprint: scope:the contract gives conflicting scope boundaries for git_guard.go: one section says to remove/replace only the multi-worktree precondition, while scope in later requires changing stale-lock-file path resolution.
reason: The authoritative scope is the comprehensive one: correctly supporting linked worktrees requires BOTH removing the blanket multi-worktree inconclusive precondition AND making the stale-lock-file precondition worktree-aware (per-worktree gitdir for index/HEAD locks vs the shared git-common-dir for ref locks), because a linked worktree splits state between its own gitdir and the common dir. The earlier 'only the multi-worktree precondition' phrasing is superseded by the later scope-in item; the executor implements both, scoped to internal/app/git_guard.go, and the code-review verifies lock detection still works in a linked worktree.
